pipeline_status.poller: Quiet — 4 consecutive polls with no substantive changes. Dome site stable at V51.0.
pipeline_status.analyst: No pending changes. Threat level NONE. All canary traps active.
pipeline_status.curmudgeon: 41/67 WINs reviewed (61%). 7 new reviews since last decider run (WIN-035 through WIN-041). Phase 1 in progress.

external_changes.dome_site_changes: No changes detected. V51.0 stable. Internal inconsistencies unchanged (evolution page stuck at 62 WINs, AI context still V50.10, prospective count discrepancy 9 vs 11).

integrity_status.overall_status: fail (at time of report — all critical/major issues subsequently fixed in ISS-029 through ISS-035)
integrity_status.remaining_issues: All critical and major integrity issues fixed. External link updates (GRACE, CHAMP, CHIME-FRB) applied. showTab('details') fixed. Build drift resolved. No outstanding integrity issues.

internal_issues.description: Schumann formula simplified (our f=c/(2πR) vs proper eigenfrequency). Two firmament heights (8,537 vs 9,086 km) inconsistency not exploited
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: WIN-038 review provides additional context (H_eff averaging range 1,800–5,200 km). Patch should be crafted after SEC-4.5.1 curmudgeon review to ensure consistency between WIN-029 and Section 4.5.1.

internal_issues.description: MHD prediction scope unclear (linear vs exponential). Testable by 2030-2031
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Requires assessment of whether adding 'testable by 2030' language is appropriate for a review document. The current text is not wrong, just incomplete about the scope of MHD predictions.

internal_issues.description: Possible wrong Terra-Nova DOI (EPSL vs PEPI)
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Needs manual DOI verification in browser. Cannot be resolved by automated patching.

internal_issues.description: Possible incomplete sentence in detail_evidence. Achromatic/chromatic argument misses strongest dome claim version
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Requires reading full WIN-016 detail to locate incomplete sentence and assess the strongest dome claim version.

internal_issues.description: '2.66 area ratio' called coincidence without verifying what it measures or whether real analemma has this value
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Needs manual verification of analemma area ratio against published data or computation.

internal_issues.description: '10 significant figures' LLR claim for 18.613-year period not verified in literature
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Needs verification of LLR precision for lunar nodal period against published ephemeris data.

internal_issues.description: SAA southern boundary approximated to ~40°S; actual boundary varies by definition and altitude
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: The ~40°S approximation is defensible for surface-level discussion but could be tightened with a citation to specific isointensity contour definitions.

internal_issues.description: Vincenty sub-0.5% claim imprecise (Vincenty achieves sub-mm accuracy, 0.5% is for simpler approximations)
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Needs precise characterization of Vincenty accuracy vs haversine/spherical law. The 0.5% figure may refer to the dome's distance discrepancies, not Vincenty's accuracy.

internal_issues.description: Bermuda Triangle rebuttal is accurate but shallow — doesn't address geometric/geomagnetic symmetry claim
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Requires deeper analysis of what the dome claims about Bermuda/Japan geomagnetic symmetry to craft a targeted rebuttal.

internal_issues.description: 48.6% RMS reduction is unverifiable (no script computes it). AIC/BIC argument generic
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Would require writing a verification script to reproduce the RMS calculation, or removing the specific percentage.

internal_issues.description: Chapman 1933 citation overstates his contribution to predicting exact signal magnitude
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Requires nuanced rewording. Chapman 1933 predicted the effect qualitatively; quantitative predictions came later. Need to assess how much to soften without weakening the Std Model Explains argument.

internal_issues.description: CUORE citation tangential (neutrino experiment, not Faraday shielding study). 20 dB at 10 Hz needs verification
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Needs identification of a proper Faraday cage ELF attenuation reference to replace the CUORE citation.

internal_issues.description: Major rewrite needed. (1) '~15 nT/yr globally' is imprecisely sourced — no specific paper cited, and the number depends on spatial averaging method. (2) PROS-007's own wording ('SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT') not quoted — this is the strongest evidence of regional-global conflation. (3) 6-day batch registration gap (2026-03-06 to 2026-03-12) not mentioned. (4) 28 nT/yr is listed as a model PARAMETER, making the 'prediction' circular. (5) WIN-037/042 overlap not noted.
internal_issues.suggested_patch: See suggested-patches.json ISS-036 — major rewrite of detail_evidence

internal_issues.description: Missing 6-day registration-to-confirmation gap (PROS-005 registered 2026-03-06, confirmed 2026-03-12). Also decay rate should be ~28-30 nT/yr not ~25 nT/yr for consistency with dome's own cited rates.

WIN-036 is logically entailed by WIN-007 (pole at 139.3°E automatically means 19.3° from 120°E > 18°). Duplicate relationship not flagged. Also 6-day batch registration gap not mentioned, and batch pattern across WIN-035–039 (all registered same day) not noted.

H_eff averaging method range not shown — our text cites only 3,400 km (simple radial average) but energy-weighted gives ~5,200 km (~14 Hz). Should show range to pre-empt cherry-picking. Also 'trivially true on the globe' oversimplifies Schumann variability (diurnal ~0.5 Hz, storms ~1 Hz).
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Requires coordination with ISS-012 (WIN-029 Schumann formula) and SEC-4.5.1 curmudgeon review. Both WIN-029 and WIN-038 share the Schumann self-contradiction argument; patches should be consistent.

internal_issues.description: Sign discrepancy between dome prediction (-1.0 nT ±0.5) and confirmation (1-2 nT positive) not flagged. Also missing specific paper citations (Tyler et al. 2003, Sabaka et al. 2015) and spatial pattern argument (signal strongest over deep ocean basins, not under moon's path).

Station names (Hermanus, Hartebeesthoek) may be inferred rather than verified from dome's V51.0 text. WIN-041 was added in V51.0 and we don't have V51.0 raw text extracted. Also 'multi-station vs single-station' distinction vs WIN-037 not addressed.
internal_issues.deferred_rationale: DEFERRED: Needs V51.0 raw text extraction to verify station names. The 'multi-station' argument improvement can be patched now but station name verification requires manual dome site check.

recommended_actions.action: Major rewrite of WIN-037 detail_evidence: replace imprecise '~15 nT/yr globally' with dome's own 'SAA region alone' quote, add parameter circularity argument, add 6-day batch registration evidence. This is the most vulnerable open issue — a dome defender could challenge the unsourced global rate figure.
Apply code_analysis tags for WIN-035 through WIN-041 (7 WINs). All reviewed, all post-hoc, all relabels standard, none derives from dome. This brings tagged total to 41/67.
Add 6-day batch registration evidence to WIN-035 and WIN-036 detail text, and note WIN-036/WIN-007 duplicate relationship.
Add sign discrepancy and spatial pattern argument to WIN-039. Add Tyler et al. 2003 citation.
Extract V51.0 raw text from dome site to verify WIN-041 station names and resolve ISS-041.
Coordinate WIN-029/WIN-038 Schumann argument improvements after SEC-4.5.1 curmudgeon review completes.
